mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The relationship is consistent across cycles.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return following a 2% monthly increase in RSAFS is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 4.5%, it indicates labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 4.5%, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High nominal interest rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with low inflation (CPIAUCSL) produce a high real interest rate, which depresses equity valuations and forward returns. The interaction of these two series, not either alone, drives the signal.
failure_condition: When real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI YoY) exceeds 2%, the 20-day forward return is not reliably negative (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust GDP growth (GDPC1 annualized > 2.5%), further positive surprises in GDP drive above-trend equity returns over 60 trading days. The signal only activates in expansion regimes.
failure_condition: In expansion regimes, the 60-day forward return following a GDP release with growth >2.5% is not significantly higher than in non-expansion periods (Mann-Whitney p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity, which is a leading indicator of economic health and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following a 0.5% monthly increase in INDPRO is not significantly positive (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) of at least 25 basis points within a month signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return after a 25bp rate cut is not positive in at least 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) confirms robust consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The joint signal is stronger than either alone.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and PAYEMS show positive 3-month growth
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when both series are growing is not significantly greater than when only one is growing (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 3%), rising housing starts (HOUST) stimulate construction activity and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days. The signal reverses in high rate regimes.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following a monthly increase in HOUST when FEDFUNDS < 3% is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10).
